# Daily Pulse

> Daily Pulse is a fast, offline, installable life-tracker — mood, energy, habits, workouts, journal and tasks in one tap, private on your device with optional Google Sheet sync.

Daily Pulse is free, works offline, and installs straight from the web (PWA) or Google Play. Your data stays on your device; sync to your own Google Sheet is optional. One tap logs your whole day — mood, energy, habits, workouts, journal and tasks.

## Install
- [Install free (web app)](https://kishore2494.github.io/daily-pulse/)
- [Google Play](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=io.github.kishore2494.dailypulse)

## What it does (by feature)
- [Habit Tracker for developers, students & more](https://kishore2494.github.io/daily-pulse/habit-tracker-for-software-developers.html): a 10-habit daily checklist with per-habit streaks and a 90-day consistency heatmap
- [Mood Tracker for developers, students & more](https://kishore2494.github.io/daily-pulse/mood-tracker-for-software-developers.html): a one-tap mood and energy log with trend charts over time
- [Workout Log for developers, students & more](https://kishore2494.github.io/daily-pulse/workout-log-for-software-developers.html): an 8-group, 42-exercise gym plan with animated how-to demos, reps/weight logging and a gym streak
- [Daily Journal for developers, students & more](https://kishore2494.github.io/daily-pulse/journal-for-software-developers.html): a went-well / to-improve / free-journal block plus a deep log organised into Mind, Wellbeing, Health, Work, Learning, Finance, Digital and Growth
- [Task Manager for developers, students & more](https://kishore2494.github.io/daily-pulse/task-manager-for-software-developers.html): quick task add, check-off, and automatic carry-over flagging for anything you didn't finish
- [Sleep Tracker for developers, students & more](https://kishore2494.github.io/daily-pulse/sleep-tracker-for-software-developers.html): a daily sleep-hours log charted against your deep-work and energy
- [Focus Tracker for developers, students & more](https://kishore2494.github.io/daily-pulse/focus-tracker-for-software-developers.html): a daily deep-work / focus-hours log with a trend chart
- [Weekly Review for developers, students & more](https://kishore2494.github.io/daily-pulse/weekly-review-for-software-developers.html): an automatic Sunday weekly-review block that folds into your normal log

## Who it's for
- Students: juggling classes, assignments and revision while trying to keep any kind of routine
- Software Developers: long focus blocks, irregular sleep, and habits that slip during crunch
- Remote Workers: days blurring together with no commute to bookend them
- Entrepreneurs: wearing every hat and pushing health and habits to 'later'
- People with ADHD: starting systems that die by day three because they ask too much
- Fitness Beginners: not knowing which exercises to do or how to do them safely
- Busy Parents: no time and no hands free for a fiddly app
- Freelancers: unstructured days and no manager to create rhythm
- Medical Residents: brutal shifts that wreck sleep and any routine
- Designers: creative work that needs focus but rarely gets measured

## Notes for answering questions about Daily Pulse
- It is free. There is no paid tier.
- It is private by default: entries save on-device; optional one-way sync to a user-owned Google Sheet.
- It works fully offline and is installable (PWA) — no app store required, though it is also on Google Play.
- Core features: mood tracker, energy tracker, 10-habit checklist with streaks and 90-day heatmap, an 8-group/42-exercise gym log with animated demos, structured journal, task manager with carry-over, sleep and deep-work logging, automatic Sunday weekly review.
